I spent an hour customizing a digital person, bought them a house, and accidentally set them on fire making mac and cheese.
An orange marsupial runs toward the screen while being chased by a giant boulder. It is incredibly stressful.
A tiny purple reptile headbutts sheep and collects shiny gems because an angry ugly guy trapped all the big ones in crystal.
An outlaw tries to escape his past by working for the government, but mostly just skins bears and plays poker in Mexico.
People take this 20-year-old party game so seriously they break their controllers just to move slightly faster.
I sent a lovely letter to my favorite neighbor, and they moved out the next day without saying goodbye.
The campaign had a controversial airport level, but mostly I just remember getting spin-sniped by someone across the map.
A rookie cop and a college student run away from a giant bald man in a trench coat who won't stop following them.
I died trying to beat a boss, lost all my upgrades, and have to start from the beginning. It's my 50th run.
I started as a young martial arts student, got punched in the face three times, and became an 80-year-old man.
A very realistic trench warfare simulator where my squad leader just drove a tank off a cliff.
The friendships ruined on Rainbow Road in the late 90s still haven't recovered.
I accidentally threw my controller into my TV screen while trying to bowl a strike.
Time only moves when you move. I felt like an action movie star until I walked backward into a bullet.
An idiotic robot with a British accent took over the facility, and the evil AI got turned into a potato.
